data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE EIGHT-child sibling roster (S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the materialization-core (primitive) slice; the union of the eight children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

Coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role):
- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER of the "faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA" half: its AC1 (materialize() byte-matches the committed tree at the resolved SHA INCLUDING export-ignore files — faithful, not git archive), AC2 (LFS pointer detection + defined/documented submodule handling), AC3 (atomic population — reader never sees a partial tree). The "no gate reads the working tree / all gates re-rooted onto the snapshot" half is S3 (fa49 AC1/AC3/AC4). Heart of this child. Clean, no gap.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed manifest channel — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2 manifest-step binding + reproducible re-run) atop the immutable-SHA basis THIS child produces (its What: "resolve ref to an immutable SHA against origin"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (it produces the SHA the signature pins but does not sign; its Scope: "Out: ... signing (S4)"). Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — covered by S3 (fa49 AC4 reads in-place + advisory AC "source=local documented rollback") + S4 (30b3 AC3 bars signing) + S6 (docs). This child only notes the local/attested fork at the fetch boundary ("attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs") and does not own the read-root/signing-bar/back-out semantics. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark — S2 — without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child owns the no-git-lock-contention + atomic/no-worktree-population half (its AC6: concurrent materialize() of different SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock; its AC3: atomic population, crashed materialization leaves only a tmp/ dir cleaned on startup); S2 (70c7) owns single-flight + delete-on-last-close; S2b (b73c) owns janitor reclamation under the free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads. No double-ownership; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER of the RAISE: its AC5 (a private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error; attested mode fails closed). S6 (1ba5 AC1/AC2) DOCUMENTS the message + remedy + fail-closed/local-still-runs. Raise + named-doc-owner pairing complete. Clean.
- Parent AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER: its AC4 (no external tar binary invoked; no hardcoded /tmp; runs on macOS + Linux) + its What portability seam (extraction never shells to tar; temp root from tempfile.gettempdir()/mkdtemp 0700, overridable via REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR, created OUTSIDE the repo/.git; fcntl.flock with atomic-mkdir fallback on a local FS). S6 documents REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the EFS/NFS flock caveat. Heart of this child's portability slice. Clean.
- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY covered by S2/S2b (free-space watermark + grace/age windows configurable with documented defaults; S2b AC9) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via the standard rebar.toml + REBAR_* precedence) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; it introduces REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR but the named tunables are owned elsewhere. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — covered by S2/S2b (b73c AC10: ADR records the cache/janitor architecture incl. content-addressed layout, delete-on-last-close, flock GC interlock AND the rejected PID+heartbeat lease) + S4 (30b3 AC1: manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision recorded in the ADR — the rejected payload-schema SHA binding). THIS child contributes the read-tree+checkout-index-over-`git archive` decision content (its Why/What) which the parent ADR note lists as captured "authored in S2/S4"; this child is not the ADR author and does not claim that AC. Both named rejected alternatives have an authoring home. Not an absence.
- Parent AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this child is S1, the primitive, story #1 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6. The "immutable, reproducible snapshot ... at a pinned SHA" + "faithful, never the mutable checkout" basis is PRECISELY this child's core deliverable (faithful read-tree+checkout-index materialization at the resolved immutable SHA, atomic population, lock-free concurrency-safe, portable). The "safely under concurrent use" clause is split with S2/S2b (cache single-flight + janitor); the "client-specified" surfacing is S5; "signed/verified_at_sha/manifest channel" is S4; "local preserves verify-before-push" is S3/S4/S6. This child introduces no behavioral obligation that any sibling fails to claim.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Scope explicitly delegates each downstream slice to a named sibling present in the roster — the cache/janitor to S2/S2b ("Out: the cache/janitor (S2)"), gate wiring to S3 ("Out: ... gate wiring (S3)"), signing to S4 ("Out: ... signing (S4)"), surfaces to S5 ("Out: ... surfaces (S5)"). The credential-message detail it raises is documented by S6 (its AC1/AC2 names this hand-off, per S6's own "see S6" cross-ref). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) / S4b (e308) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4/S4b seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap, and is downstream of this materialization-core child's resolved-SHA output. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, the ref-coherence clause now owned by S4b) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them; the WHO-threads-it question lives in the G4 channel.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the materialization-core slice (faithful read-tree+checkout-index tree at the resolved immutable SHA incl. export-ignore; LFS/submodule handling; atomic all-or-nothing population + startup tmp/ cleanup; tar-free + no-hardcoded-/tmp + flock+mkdir-fallback portability; coalesced fetch; descriptive fail-closed credential error; no index.lock/config.lock contention on concurrent different-SHA materialize) of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half of parent AC1, the no-git-lock-contention + atomic-population half of AC4, the raise half of AC5, and the portability AC6. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
